data.title: The Bull Case For Bank of Communications (SEHK:3328) Could Change Following Diverging Net Income And EPS Trends
data.description:
• Bank of Communications Co., Ltd. has released its full-year 2025 results, reporting higher net interest income and net income, and has proposed at its upcoming June 26, 2026 AGM a final cash dividend of RMB 1.684 per 10 shares (inclusive of tax), totaling RMB 14.88 billion, payable in July and August 2026 to eligible A- and H-shareholders.
  • An interesting feature of these announcements is that net income increased while both basic and diluted earnings per share from continuing operations fell to RMB 1.08, highlighting the impact of a larger share base on per-share profitability even as aggregate earnings rose.
  • With the proposed final dividend and the tension between higher net income and lower earnings per share, we’ll assess how this shapes Bank of Communications’ investment narrative.

What Is Bank of Communications' Investment Narrative?

To own Bank of Communications, you need to be comfortable with a large, systemically important Chinese bank that is prioritising balance sheet strength, steady (if unspectacular) profit growth and regular cash returns. The 2025 results reinforce that story: net interest income and net income rose, but earnings per share fell as the much larger share base from last year’s sizeable equity issuance diluted per‑share profitability. The proposed final dividend of RMB 1.684 per 10 shares, on top of the recent interim payout, suggests management still wants the stock to appeal to income‑oriented investors, even as total dividends now have to be spread across roughly RMB 88.36 billion of share capital. Near term, the key catalysts and risks remain the same, but the dilution makes per‑share growth and dividend sustainability more central to the investment case.
